title: bool expressions should be avoided as operands to built-in operators other than =, &&, ||, !, ==, !=, unary &, and the conditional operator
description: "bool" expressions should not be used as operands to built-in operators other than =, &&, ||, !, ==, !=, unary &, and the conditional operatorAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misrasuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe use ofbooloperands with other operators is unlikely to be meaningful (or intended). Best case it will be confusing to maintainers, worst case it will not have the intended effect. Either way, it is highly recommended to stick to boolean operators when dealing withbooloperands.This rule allows the detection of such uses, which often occur because the logical operators (&&,||and!) can be easily confused with the bitwise operators (&,|and~).Noncompliant code examplebool b1 = true; bool b2 = false; int8_t s8a; if ( b1 & b2 ) // Nonco
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
bool b1 = true, b2 = false;
int result = b1 & b2;     // Noncompliant: bitwise AND on bool operands

bool flag = true;
int shifted = flag << 2;    // Noncompliant: left shift on bool operand

bool a = true, b = false;
int sum = a + b;          // Noncompliant: arithmetic addition on bool operands

bool condition = false;
int negated = ~condition;   // Noncompliant: bitwise NOT on bool operand

bool x = true, y = false;
int xor_result = x ^ y;   // Noncompliant: bitwise XOR on bool operands

bool enabled = true;
int doubled = enabled * 2;   // Noncompliant: multiplication on bool operand
compliant:
bool b1 = true, b2 = false;
bool result = b1 && b2;    // Compliant: logical AND

bool flag = true;
bool assigned = flag;        // Compliant: assignment operator

bool a = true, b = false;
bool or_result = a || b;     // Compliant: logical OR

bool condition = false;
bool negated = !condition;    // Compliant: logical NOT

bool x = true, y = false;
bool equal = (x == y);      // Compliant: equality comparison

bool enabled = true;
bool not_equal = (enabled != false); // Compliant: inequality comparison

bool flag = true;
bool* ptr = &flag;           // Compliant: address-of operator

bool condition = true;
int result = condition ? 10 : 5; // Compliant: conditional operator

checks.message: Bool expressions should be avoided as operands to built-in operators other than =, &&, ||, !, ==, !=, unary &, and the conditional operator.
